planning_rule: All planning source-of-truth files live in docs/plans as JSON. Do not add Markdown planning files.

operator_go_policy.standing_permission: Continue repo_only and safe_offline implementation for backend, Godot interface, and Steam preparation without requiring a fresh operator Go.
allowed_without_fresh_go: Rust backend/server scaffolding, protocol parsing, fixture validation, deterministic local tests, fake transports, local loopback networking, and offline harnesses.

Godot GDScript interface/adapters, protocol codecs, command queues, snapshot buffers, client world state, render adapter stubs, local UI wiring needed for testability, and headless/check-only validation.

Steam facade contracts, lobby-state documentation, local/mock Steam adapters, Spacewar-safe configuration placeholders, endpoint advertisement models, and dedicated-server handshake interfaces that do not call live Steam services.
Docs, runbooks, evidence templates, local scripts, CI/local matrix work, redacted logs, and machine-readable plan progress updates.
requires_explicit_fresh_go: Real Steam auth/session ticket validation or any call that mutates/depends on live Steam state.
Two-machine live Steam smoke or release-candidate live validation.
Downloads, installers, credentials, secrets, deploys, public network mutation, destructive Git, force-push, or unrelated cleanup.
Final product/design choices for rendering technology, long-term ECS crate lock-in, faction scale, art return, movement model, or gameplay balance.
operator_go_policy.testing_note: The operator may be unable to run tests early. Codex should still implement safe repo_only slices with local checks where available, record missing live/manual checks as gates, and continue to the next safe slice.
operator_go_policy.memory_note: Future Codex runs should treat this as standing permission for safe backend, Godot interface, and Steam preparation work until the operator changes it; live Steam, secrets, installers, deploys, destructive Git, and final product/design decisions still require explicit fresh Go.

parallel_execution.rule: Parallel workers may run only on disjoint allowed paths and must not mark a slice done without implementation, checks or explicit check deferral, and documentation/evidence.
safe_execution_order: Use foundation_execution.current_slice and each handoff's next_recommended_slice as the serial integration order.
Within that order, backend_protocol, godot_interface, steam_preparation, and evidence_and_build may run in parallel only when their allowed paths are disjoint and dependencies are already satisfied.
If a lane hits needs_live_go or needs_design, document the gate and continue another repo_only or safe_offline lane rather than waiting.
Steam preparation may progress through STEAM-01 and STEAM-02 after LOBBY and RECON dependencies, but STEAM-03 and any real ticket/session/AppID validation stay blocked until explicit fresh Go.

blocked_by: live deploy or public network mutation

goal: Create a real client/server RTS foundation where Godot handles rendering, input, UI, Steam lobby flow, and client prediction feedback, while a dedicated Rust server owns authoritative simulation for very large entity counts.

foundation_decisions.default: GDScript first; no C# requirement for the first spike
Custom UDP behind a transport abstraction

phases.name: Foundation And Decisions
phases.done_state: Architecture defaults, gates, and protocol draft are explicit.

phases.name: Godot Client Foundation
phases.done_state: Godot folder, scene, node, bridge, and logging contracts are explicit.

phases.name: Minimal Network Loop
phases.done_state: Two local clients can connect to one server and render authoritative snapshots.

phases.name: Resilience
phases.done_state: Ack/resend, loss recovery, reconnect, replay diagnostics, soak stability, overload behavior, and hardening work.

phases.name: Gameplay-As-Test Planning
phases.done_state: Gameplay handoff becomes scenario contracts and faction-scale load profiles.

phases.name: Scale And Performance
phases.done_state: Stress gates, concrete budgets, movement scale, authoritative map data, CI checks, and historical trend evidence exist.

phases.name: Playtest Gameplay Loop
phases.done_state: Abstract 2-player RTS loop validates controls, cover, capture economy, and authority.

phases.name: Swarm And Role Expansion
phases.done_state: Unit-role mix and zombie pressure validate large multi-faction simulation.

phases.name: Production Match Flow
phases.done_state: Steam lobby to dedicated-server flow, runbooks, release evidence, RC gates, and art-return gates exist.

verification_gates.requirement: Protocol fixtures encode/decode on Rust and Godot sides.
Two local Godot clients connect to one local dedicated server.
Server simulates at least 1,000 abstract entities.
Clients render only authoritative snapshot state.
Reconnect restores the same player session with a full snapshot.
Packet loss does not cause double-spend, double-spawn, or stale state authority.
1k, 5k, and 10k performance harnesses produce tick/render/network metrics.
Steam lobby ID copy/join/ready flow remains testable.
Same-workstation multi-client smoke passes for each architecture change.
Real two-machine Steam smoke is required for release candidates.
Two-player abstract RTS loop supports select/move/attack/take-cover, HQ spawn, credits, capture points, and late swarm trigger.
Faction-scale scenarios measure per-faction unit counts, zombie pressure, AOI bandwidth, server tick, and Godot render budget.
Gameplay additions cannot bypass server authority or command idempotency.
Every substantial Godot feature has a documented folder, scene, node/subnode tree, Resource contract, and dynamic-node exception list before implementation.
Godot server bridge exposes logs/counters for connect, handshake, command seq/ack, snapshot decode/apply, interpolation, AOI, reconnect, and packet loss.
Performance ledger records trend rows for server tick, snapshot size, bandwidth, Godot decode/apply/render, memory, visible entities, and reconnect full snapshot time.
Provisional performance budgets exist for server tick p95/p99, client frame p95, bandwidth, memory, reconnect full snapshot time, and test hardware baseline before 5k/10k claims.
Deterministic server replay can reproduce command streams, compare golden snapshots/checksums, and identify the first divergent tick for desync investigations.
Soak and chaos runs cover long sessions, reconnect churn, packet loss, jitter, slow clients, memory growth, queue depth, log volume, and graceful shutdown.
Backpressure rules cap command rate, snapshot backlog, per-client bandwidth, pending reliable commands, and log volume with explicit degrade/drop/disconnect behavior.
Large-army movement has measured pathfinding, formation, avoidance, and swarm movement scenarios that do not regress server tick budgets.
Server-authoritative map, cover, obstacle, spawn, capture, and navigation data are exported/versioned from authored content and checksum-verified by client and server.
Protocol and server hardening tests reject malformed packets, oversized messages, stale/replayed commands, version mismatches, and abusive rates without crash or authority leak.
CI/local build gates pin toolchains, run protocol fixtures, Rust tests, Godot headless checks, perf smoke, and produce reproducible evidence artifacts.

stop_rules: Stop on secrets, tokens, private content, or raw provider/session data being persisted.
Stop on destructive Git needs, force-push, reset, or unrelated staged files.
Stop on live Steam/provider/server mutation without explicit Go.
Stop on hot-file conflicts between agents.
Stop if a slice would copy prototype gameplay/assets into Millions.
Stop if architecture changes break either local multi-client mode or Steam lobby mode without an explicit gate.
Stop if a slice claims a scale or stability milestone without budget, replay/diagnostic, soak, and performance-ledger evidence for the touched path.
Stop if a change hides overload by unbounded queues, unbounded logs, or unlimited per-client bandwidth instead of defining degrade/drop behavior.

risks.gap: Transport choice is open.
risks.decision: Use a transport abstraction immediately; first implementation is custom UDP.

risks.gap: Binary protocol is conceptual only.
risks.decision: Define protocol_v0 before server/client code.

risks.gap: Steam lobby to dedicated-server session mapping is undefined.
risks.decision: Steam remains identity/lobby/discovery; server owns player_session_id.

risks.gap: Interest management is not concrete.
risks.decision: Use spatial grid/AOI subscription from the first 1k server spike.

risks.gap: Reconnect can be bolted on too late.
risks.decision: Reconnect is part of the first spike.

risks.gap: Godot may render too many rich nodes.
risks.decision: First renderer uses simple batched proxies and records render budget.

risks.gap: Multi-client test setup can diverge from Steam tests.
risks.decision: Maintain local direct server mode and Steam lobby mode.

risks.gap: 10k entity scale can become a late surprise.
risks.decision: Add automated 1k, 5k, and 10k performance gates before real gameplay.

risks.gap: Rust server crate choices may cause churn.
risks.decision: Keep server module boundaries independent of ECS and transport crates.

risks.gap: Protocol/schema drift between Godot and Rust.
risks.decision: Generate or validate shared packet fixtures.

risks.gap: Authority rules may leak into UI/client code.
risks.decision: Client code talks only to network adapter and renderer-state adapter.

risks.gap: Steam AppID ambiguity can invalidate tests.
risks.decision: Keep AppID configurable and Spacewar fallback only.

risks.gap: Gameplay scope can overtake infrastructure.
risks.decision: Gameplay is the first test target and load profile, not first implementation layer.

risks.gap: Prototype gameplay may be accidentally copied.
risks.decision: Treat TankGame behavior as requirements and test inspiration only.

risks.gap: Huge units for all factions can be undefined.
risks.decision: Define faction-scale scenarios with role mix and per-client AOI.

risks.gap: Zombie horde can destroy budgets.
risks.decision: Swarm is a dedicated load profile with gradual spawn, AI LOD, and aggregate far-state replication.

risks.gap: Asset/style work can distort readability tests.
risks.decision: Use abstract readable shapes until playtest loop and performance gates are stable.

risks.gap: Godot can drift into code-built scene structure.
risks.decision: Every reusable/fixed Godot feature needs planned folder, tscn, node tree, and ownership boundary.

risks.gap: Godot server bridge can mix with UI/gameplay code.
risks.decision: Add dedicated Godot server bridge modules.

risks.gap: Performance history can be lost.
risks.decision: Maintain a performance ledger from first harness.

risks.gap: Debugging large multiplayer state can be blind.
risks.decision: Build structured logging and observability early.

risks.gap: Performance goals can stay too vague.
risks.decision: Define provisional p95/p99 budgets, baseline hardware, bandwidth, memory, client frame, server tick, and reconnect thresholds.

risks.gap: Simulation bugs and desyncs may be unreproducible.
risks.decision: Record deterministic command streams and replay them server-side.

risks.gap: Short smoke tests can miss long-run failures.
risks.decision: Add soak and chaos scenarios.

risks.gap: Overload can create unbounded queues, bandwidth, or logs.
risks.decision: Define explicit caps, backpressure, snapshot drop/degrade, command rate limits, and slow-client thresholds.

risks.gap: Large-army movement can become hidden dominant cost.
risks.decision: Treat movement/pathfinding/formations/avoidance as dedicated scale roadmap.

risks.gap: Godot-authored map state can drift from server authority.
risks.decision: Export/version/checksum map, cover, obstacle, spawn, capture, and nav data.

risks.gap: Malformed or abusive traffic can crash or corrupt state.
risks.decision: Add protocol hardening, fuzz/property tests, packet limits, stale/replay rejection, and redacted diagnostics.

risks.gap: Builds and evidence can become non-reproducible.
risks.decision: Pin toolchains, define CI/local matrix, preserve lockfile policy, and export comparable evidence artifacts.

Public exposure, live security coverage, real Steam auth, raw packet diagnostics, continuous fuzzing, gameplay payload semantics, and release-candidate hardening claims remain gated.

gates.blocks: release-candidate evidence and trustworthy performance history

integration.rule: Gameplay handoff becomes the first real validation target for infrastructure, not a replacement for the infrastructure roadmap.
hard_rules: No gameplay slice is done unless it preserves server authority.
No gameplay slice is done unless command idempotency is preserved.
No gameplay slice is done unless local multi-client testability remains.
No gameplay slice is done unless future Steam testability remains.
No gameplay slice is done without performance measurement for touched systems.
No movement-heavy gameplay proceeds before movement-scale evidence.
No cover/obstacle/spawn/capture/navigation gameplay proceeds without map-data version/checksum authority.
No broader external playtest proceeds before protocol hardening coverage.
No release-candidate claim proceeds without pinned build/toolchain and comparable evidence artifacts.
faction_scale_stages: Stage A: 2 player factions, neutral capture systems, zombie faction.
Stage B: 2 player factions plus AI-controlled pressure groups.
Stage C: 4+ simulated factions with mixed role composition.
Stage D: 10,000+ total entities with AOI, LOD, aggregate far state, and measured bandwidth.

performance_budgets.claim_rule: Scale, reconnect, soak, and release-candidate claims remain blocked unless the report includes the relevant budget key, measured value, machine label, tool versions, and pass/fail result.
